positional_bias.note: max_share == 0.40 is not > 0.40; threshold is exclusive. Sidecar positions [0,1,2,3,0] confirmed against .gift — no desync. No auto-correction needed.

length_bias.note: Q2 correct answer (103 chars) marginally exceeds distractor_mean * 1.20 (102.40) by 0.6 chars. The extra length is structurally necessary: the stem asks for size AND row-meaning in a single question (dual-mapped LO2+LO3), so the correct answer must address both clauses. Only 1 of 5 questions is longest-correct; rate = 0.20, well under the 0.40 fail threshold. Feedback text stripped before all measurements.

distractor_plausibility.note: q1-D3 ('set of instructions for neural network learning') and q4-D1 ('list of 784 pixel labels in alphabetical order') score 2 — wrong but not aligned to a common misconception. Neither is absurd (score 1). All other distractors score 3 or 4. absurd_rate = 0.00.

corpus_los: LO1: Describe what a matrix is in plain language — a rectangular grid of scalars arranged in rows and columns.
LO2: Read standard matrix notation, including how to state its size using rows x columns.
LO3: Identify a row in a matrix as a feature vector that describes one data sample.
LO4: Explain how AI uses matrices to store images, representing each pixel value as one cell in a grid.
LO5: Recognise the term 'weight matrix' and describe, in one sentence, why neural networks use one.
LO6: Name four advanced matrix operations — multiplication, transpose, determinant, eigenvalues — and state that each is covered in a later topic.

dual_map_accepted.question: q2
dual_map_accepted.rationale: Q2's objective_ref explicitly states both LO2 ('rows x columns notation') and LO3 ('each row is one feature vector — one data sample'). The stem asks for BOTH the matrix size AND what one row represents. The correct answer states '200 x 6' (satisfying LO2) AND 'each row holds one customer's complete feature values — making it a feature vector' (satisfying LO3). Similarity to each LO exceeds the 0.70 threshold. Dual-mapping accepted per stated skill rule: objective_ref fuzzy-match >= 0.70 against the LO text.

objective_coverage.note: All six LOs covered. LO3 coverage restored by Q2 redesign without adding a sixth question. No orphan questions.

beginner_fairness.note: All stems use plain language consistent with the corpus. 'Feature vector' appears in Q2 — defined in corpus §5.3 and listed as prerequisite from topic 6.3 in corpus §2; not jargon for this audience. Q3 names matrix multiplication, transpose, determinant, and eigenvalues — all explicitly introduced and scoped to later topics in corpus §5.6. No out-of-scope knowledge required to answer any question correctly.

uncovered: LO6: Name four advanced matrix operations and state each is covered in a later topic.
cycle_history.fix_applied: Q3 replaced — new LO6 recognition question asking learner to identify all four deferred operations.

uncovered: LO3: Identify a row in a matrix as a feature vector that describes one data sample.
cycle_history.fix_applied: Q2 redesigned: stem asks for size AND row meaning; correct answer explicitly names the row as a feature vector; objective_ref updated to dual-map LO2 + LO3.

verdict: Pass — all checks clean. positional_bias max_share=0.40 (boundary, not exceeded). length_bias longest_correct_rate=0.20 (Q2 marginally longest-correct by 0.6 chars, structurally justified by dual-LO stem; rate below 0.40 threshold). distractor absurd_rate=0.00. objective_coverage: all six LOs covered — Q2 dual-maps LO2 (rows x cols notation) and LO3 (row as feature vector); stem and correct answer both explicitly test both concepts; fuzzy similarity to each LO exceeds 0.70. Beginner-fairness clean. No orphan questions.
